trainingPrinciples: Progressive overload: Gradually increase weight, reps, or intensity to build strength.
Recovery: Allow adequate rest between workouts for muscle repair.
Nutrition: Fuel your body with balanced meals including protein, carbs, and fats.
Consistency: Regular training is key to achieving fitness goals.

beginner: Full body workouts 3 times per week.
Focus on compound movements like squats, push-ups, and rows.
advanced: Specialized splits like chest/tris, back/bis, legs, shoulders.
Include advanced techniques like drop sets or supersets.

safetyNotes: Use proper form to prevent injury.
Consult a doctor before starting if you have health concerns.
Stop if you feel pain (beyond normal muscle fatigue).

injuryLimitations.kneePain: Avoid deep squats; focus on leg presses or lunges with lighter weight.
injuryLimitations.backPain: Skip deadlifts; emphasize core stability exercises.
injuryLimitations.shoulderInjury: Avoid overhead presses; use chest presses instead.
